003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.json;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.bean.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
028import java.io.*;
029
030import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json5.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
034
035/**
036 * Parses any valid RFC 8259 JSON text into a POJO model.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * This parser strictly enforces RFC 8259 JSON syntax. Double-quoted strings only; single-quoted strings,
040 * comments, trailing commas, and unquoted keys are rejected. For JSON5 syntax (single quotes, comments,
041 * etc.), use {@link Json5Parser}.
042 *
043 * <h5 class='topic'>Media types</h5>
044 * <p>
045 * Handles <c>Content-Type</c> types:  <bc>application/json, text/json, application/jcs+json</bc>
046 *
047 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
048 * <p>
049 * This parser uses a state machine, which makes it very fast and efficient.  It parses JSON in about 70% of the
050 * time that it takes the built-in Java DOM parsers to parse equivalent XML.
051 *
052 * <p>
053 * This parser handles all valid RFC 8259 JSON syntax.
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * Also handles negative, decimal, hexadecimal, octal, and double numbers, including exponential notation.
057 *
058 * <p>
059 * This parser handles the following input, and automatically returns the corresponding Java class.
060 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
061 *    <li>
062 *       JSON objects (<js>"{...}"</js>) are converted to {@link JsonMap JsonMaps}.
063 *       <b>Note:</b>  If a <code><xa>_type</xa>=<xs>'xxx'</xs></code> attribute is specified on the object, then an
064 *       attempt is made to convert the object to an instance of the specified Java bean class.
065 *       See the {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContext.Builder#typePropertyName(String)} setting for more information about parsing
066 *       beans from JSON.
067 *    <li>
068 *       JSON arrays (<js>"[...]"</js>) are converted to {@link JsonList JsonLists}.
069 *    <li>
070 *       JSON string literals (<js>"\"xyz\""</js>) are converted to {@link String Strings}.
071 *    <li>
072 *       JSON numbers (<js>"123"</js>, including octal/hexadecimal/exponential notation) are converted to
073 *       {@link Integer Integers}, {@link Long Longs}, {@link Float Floats}, or {@link Double Doubles} depending on
074 *       whether the number is decimal, and the size of the number.
075 *    <li>
076 *       JSON booleans (<js>"false"</js>) are converted to {@link Boolean Booleans}.
077 *    <li>
078 *       JSON nulls (<js>"null"</js>) are converted to <jk>null</jk>.
079 * </ul>
080 *
081 * <p>
082 * Input can be any of the following:
083 * <ul class='spaced-list'>
084 *    <li>
085 *       <js>"{...}"</js> - Converted to an {@link JsonMap} or an instance of a Java bean if a <xa>_type</xa>
086 *       attribute is present.
087 *    <li>
088 *       <js>"[...]"</js> - Converted to an {@link JsonList}.
089 *    <li>
090 *       <js>"123..."</js> - Converted to a {@link Number} (either {@link Integer}, {@link Long}, {@link Float},
091 *       or {@link Double}).
092 *    <li>
093 *       <js>"true"</js>/<js>"false"</js> - Converted to a {@link Boolean}.
094 *    <li>
095 *       <js>"null"</js> - Returns <jk>null</jk>.
096 *    <li>
097 *       <js>"\"xxx\""</js> - Converted to a {@link String} (double quotes only).
098 * </ul>
099 *
100 * <p>
101 * TIP:  If you know you're parsing a JSON object or array, it can be easier to parse it using the
102 * {@link JsonMap#JsonMap(CharSequence) JsonMap(CharSequence)} or {@link JsonList#JsonList(CharSequence)
103 * JsonList(CharSequence)} constructors instead of using this class.
104 * The end result should be the same.
105 *
106 * <h5 class='figure'>Example input (Map of name/age):</h5>
107 * <p class='bjson'>
108 *    {<js>"name"</js>:<js>"Alice"</js>,<js>"age"</js>:30}
109 * </p>
110 *
111 * <h5 class='figure'>Complex (nested object + array):</h5>
112 * <p class='bjson'>
113 *    {<js>"name"</js>:<js>"Alice"</js>,<js>"age"</js>:30,<js>"address"</js>:{<js>"street"</js>:<js>"123 Main St"</js>,<js>"city"</js>:<js>"Boston"</js>,<js>"state"</js>:<js>"MA"</js>},<js>"tags"</js>:[<js>"a"</js>,<js>"b"</js>,<js>"c"</js>]}
114 * </p>
115 *
116 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
117 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
118 * </ul>
119 *
120 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
121 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonBasics">JSON Basics</a>
122 *    <li class='link'>{@link Json5Parser} - For JSON5 syntax (single quotes, comments, etc.)
123 * </ul>
124 */
125@SuppressWarnings({
126   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable
127   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
128   "resource"   // parseTokens(...) returns a Closeable owned by the caller; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
129})
130public class JsonParser extends ReaderParser implements JsonMetaProvider, TokenReadable, ArrayRecordReadable {
131
132   // Argument name constants for assertArgNotNull
133   private static final String ARG_copyFrom = "copyFrom";
134
135   /**
136    * Builder class.
137    *
138    * <p>
139    * Unlike the abstract context builders, this builder is concrete <i>and</i> generic: it terminates the CRTP
140    * recursion via the leaf-free {@code new Builder<>()} pattern (see {@link JsonParser#create()} /
141    * {@link JsonParser#copy()}) rather than via a separate concrete {@code DefaultBuilder} leaf.
142    */
143   @SuppressWarnings({
144      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
145   })
146   public static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends ReaderParser.Builder<SELF> {
147
148      private static final Cache<HashKey,JsonParser> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, JsonParser.class).build();
149
150      private boolean validateEnd;
151
152      /**
153       * Constructor, default settings.
154       */
155      protected Builder() {
156         consumes("application/json,text/json,application/jcs+json");
157         validateEnd = env("JsonParser.validateEnd", false);
158      }
159
160      /**
161       * Copy constructor.
162       *
163       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
164       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
165       */
166      protected Builder(Builder<?> copyFrom) {
167         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
168         validateEnd = copyFrom.validateEnd;
169      }
170
171      /**
172       * Copy constructor.
173       *
174       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
175       *    <br>Cannot be <jk>null</jk>.
176       */
177      protected Builder(JsonParser copyFrom) {
178         super(assertArgNotNull(ARG_copyFrom, copyFrom));
179         validateEnd = copyFrom.validateEnd;
180      }
181
182      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
183      public JsonParser build() {
184         return cache(CACHE).build(JsonParser.class);
185      }
186
187      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
188      @SuppressWarnings({
189         "unchecked" // CRTP self-type cast on the leaf-free Builder copy is safe by construction.
190      })
191      public SELF copy() {
192         return (SELF) new Builder<>(this);
193      }
194
195      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
196      public HashKey hashKey() {
197         return HashKey.of(super.hashKey(), validateEnd);
198      }
199
200
201      /**
202       * Validate end.
203       *
204       * <p>
205       * When enabled, after parsing a POJO from the input, verifies that the remaining input in
206       * the stream consists of only comments or whitespace.
207       *
208       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
209       * <p class='bjava'>
210       *    <jc>// Create a parser that validates that there's no garbage at the end of the input.</jc>
211       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.
212       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
213       *       .validateEnd()
214       *       .build();
215       *
216       *    <jc>// Should fail because input has multiple POJOs.</jc>
217       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <js>"{foo:'bar'}{baz:'qux'}"</js>;
218       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> =<jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
219       * </p>
220       *
221       * <p>
222       * <b>Token streams:</b> this setting is not honored by {@link #parseTokens(Object) parseTokens}
223       * &mdash; the cursor leaves end-of-input checking to the caller.
224       *
225       * @return This object.
226       */
227      public SELF validateEnd() {
228         return validateEnd(true);
229      }
230
231      /**
232       * Same as {@link #validateEnd()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
233       *
234       * @param value The value for this setting.
235       * @return This object.
236       */
237      public SELF validateEnd(boolean value) {
238         validateEnd = value;
239         return self();
240      }
241   }
242
243   /** Default parser, all default settings.*/
244   public static final JsonParser DEFAULT = new JsonParser(create());
245
246   /**
247    * Creates a new builder for this object.
248    *
249    * @return A new builder.
250    */
251   @SuppressWarnings({
252      "java:S1452" // Self-typed builder: Builder<?> is the only non-raw, leaf-free return; chaining + build() unaffected.
253   })
254   public static Builder<?> create() {
255      return new Builder<>();
256   }
257
258   protected final boolean validateEnd;
259
260   private final Map<BeanPropertyMeta,JsonBeanPropertyMeta> jsonBeanPropertyMetas = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
261   private final Map<ClassMeta<?>,JsonClassMeta> jsonClassMetas = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
262   
263
264   /**
265    * Constructor.
266    *
267    * @param builder The builder for this object.
268    */
269   public JsonParser(Builder<?> builder) {
270      super(builder);
271      validateEnd = builder.validateEnd;
272   }
273
274   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
275   public Builder<?> copy() {
276      return new Builder<>(this);
277   }
278
279   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
280   public JsonParserSession.Builder<?> createSession() {
281      return JsonParserSession.create(this);
282   }
283
284   @Override /* Overridden from JsonMetaProvider */
285   public JsonBeanPropertyMeta getJsonBeanPropertyMeta(BeanPropertyMeta bpm) {
286      if (bpm == null)
287         return JsonBeanPropertyMeta.DEFAULT;
288      return jsonBeanPropertyMetas.computeIfAbsent(bpm, k -> new JsonBeanPropertyMeta(k.getDelegateFor(), this));
289   }
290
291   @Override /* Overridden from JsonMetaProvider */
292   public JsonClassMeta getJsonClassMeta(ClassMeta<?> cm) {
293      return jsonClassMetas.computeIfAbsent(cm, k -> new JsonClassMeta(k, this));
294   }
295
296   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
297   public JsonParserSession getSession() { return createSession().build(); }
298
299   /**
300    * Convenience delegator that opens a {@link JsonTokenReader} over the input using
301    * <b>default session arguments</b> (mirrors {@link #parse(Object, Class)}).
302    *
303    * <p>
304    * The real implementation lives on {@link JsonParserSession#parseTokens(Object)}.  Callers
305    * that need request-derived configuration (locale, timezone, schema, swaps) should call
306    * {@link #createSession()} and invoke {@link JsonParserSession#parseTokens(Object)} on the
307    * built session instead.
308    *
309    * @param input The input.  Accepts {@link Reader}, {@link CharSequence}, {@link InputStream},
310    *    <code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>, or {@link File}.
311    * @return A new {@link JsonTokenReader}.
312    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
313    */
314   @Override /* TokenReadable */
315   public TokenReader parseTokens(Object input) throws IOException {
316      return getSession().parseTokens(input);
317   }
318
319   /**
320    * Validate end.
321    *
322    * @see Builder#validateEnd()
323    * @return
324    *    <jk>true</jk> if after parsing a POJO from the input, verifies that the remaining input in
325    *    the stream consists of only comments or whitespace.
326    */
327   protected final boolean isValidateEnd() { return validateEnd; }
328
329   /**
330    * Convenience delegator for the streaming array-element {@link RecordReader} (uses default
331    * session args; see {@link #parseTokens(Object)}).  Real impl on
332    * {@link JsonParserSession#parseArrayRecords(Object)}.
333    *
334    * @param input The input.
335    * @return A new element-streamed {@link RecordReader}.
336    * @throws IOException If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.
337    */
338   @Override /* ArrayRecordReadable */
339   public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException {
340      return getSession().parseArrayRecords(input);
341   }
342}
